meta.summary: OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default ChatGPT model, pairing a public product launch with a system card and positioning it around lower hallucination rates, clearer answers, and stronger personalization. Amazon added OS Level Actions to Bedrock AgentCore Browser, extending agents from web-page interaction to direct on-screen OS control via the InvokeBrowser API. Google also upgraded Gemini for Home to Gemini 3.1, enabling more complex multi-step smart-home commands and signaling continued competition to own the consumer AI assistant layer.

source_sections.label: Research Papers
source_sections.description: Research papers and paper-trending signals tied to the current day.

items.title: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces
items.summary_short: Adapting large pretrained models to diverse tasks is now routine, yet the two dominant strategies of parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) and low-rank compression are typically composed in sequence.

topic_refs.headline: Compress Then Adapt? No, Do It Together via Task-aware Union of Subspaces

items.title: Component-Aware Self-Speculative Decoding in Hybrid Language Models
items.summary_short: Speculative decoding accelerates autoregressive inference by drafting candidate tokens with a fast model and verifying them in parallel with the target. Self-speculative methods avoid the need for an external drafter but have been studied exclusively in homogeneous Transformer architectures.

topic_refs.headline: Component-Aware Self-Speculative Decoding in Hybrid Language Models

items.title: When Attention Collapses: Residual Evidence Modeling for Compositional Inference
items.summary_short: Compositional inference - the decomposition of observations into an unknown number of latent components - is central to perception and scientific data analysis. Attention-based models perform well when components are approximately separable, as in object-centric vision.

items.title: The Right Answer, the Wrong Direction: Why Transformers Fail at Counting and How to Fix It
items.summary_short: Large language models often fail at simple counting tasks, even when the items to count are explicitly present in the prompt.

items.title: Task Vector Geometry Underlies Dual Modes of Task Inference in Transformers
items.summary_short: Transformers are effective at inferring the latent task from context via two inference modes: recognizing a task seen during training, and adapting to a novel one.

items.title: Stochastic Sparse Attention for Memory-Bound Inference
items.summary_short: Autoregressive decoding becomes bandwidth-limited at long contexts, as generating each token requires reading all $n_k$ key and value vectors from KV cache.

items.title: Rethinking the Rank Threshold for LoRA Fine-Tuning
items.summary_short: A recent landscape analysis of LoRA fine-tuning in the neural tangent kernel regime establishes a sufficient condition $r(r+1)/2 > KN$ on the LoRA rank $r$ for the absence of spurious local minima under squared-error loss, prescribing $r \geq 12$ on canonical few-shot RoBERTa setups.

items.title: Provable Accuracy Collapse in Embedding-Based Representations under Dimensionality Mismatch
items.summary_short: Embedding-based representations in Euclidean space $\mathbb{R}^d$ are a cornerstone of modern machine learning, where a major goal is to use the \emph{smallest dimension} that faithfully captures data relations.

items.title: Probe-Geometry Alignment: Erasing the Cross-Sequence Memorization Signature Below Chance
items.summary_short: Recent attacks show that behavioural unlearning of large language models leaves internal traces recoverable by adversarial probes. We characterise where this retention lives and show it can be surgically removed without measurable capability cost.

items.title: Nora: Normalized Orthogonal Row Alignment for Scalable Matrix Optimizer
items.summary_short: Matrix-based optimizers have demonstrated immense potential in training Large Language Models (LLMs), however, designing an ideal optimizer remains a formidable challenge.

items.title: Most ReLU Networks Admit Identifiable Parameters
items.summary_short: We study the realization map of deep ReLU networks, focusing on when a function determines its parameters up to scaling and permutation. To analyze hidden redundancies beyond these standard symmetries, we introduce a framework based on weighted polyhedral complexes.

items.title: MEMAUDIT: An Exact Package-Oracle Evaluation Protocol for Budgeted Long-Term LLM Memory Writing
items.summary_short: Long-term LLM agents must compress streams of past interactions into persistent memory before future queries are known. Existing evaluations usually measure final question-answering accuracy, which entangles memory writing with retrieval, prompting, and reader reasoning.
tags: Memory Structures and Agent Memory Systems

featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The announcement marks a major new model release from the company
featured_topics.why_it_matters: This product release surfaced across 1 independent source(s).

ByteDance Seed released the Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview model on Arena as a preview of its upcoming capabilities.

Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview Model Release on Arena

featured_topics.headline: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser Adds OS Level Actions via InvokeBrowser API
featured_topics.summary_short: AWS introduced OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser. The new capability gives agents direct operating-system control through the InvokeBrowser API so they can interact with content beyond standard browser surfaces
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Amazon is expanding Bedrock agents from browser-only automation to fuller desktop-style control, which materially broadens the range of enterprise workflows agents can execute.
key_takeaways: AWS said the new OS Level Actions capability exposes direct operating-system control through the InvokeBrowser API, allowing agents to interact with content visible on screen rather than only the browser DOM.
The launch targets production agent use cases where critical UI elements are inaccessible through standard web-layer automation, a common blocker for enterprise workflow execution.
The same Bedrock AgentCore push also highlighted AgentCore Identity as a standalone service for securing agent access across Amazon ECS, Amazon EKS, AWS Lambda, and on-premises environments.

evidence.title: Intelligence-driven message defense and insights using Amazon Bedrock
Secure AI agents with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Identity on Amazon ECS

featured_topics.headline: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT’s New Default Model
featured_topics.summary_short: OpenAI announced GPT-5.5 Instant as an update to ChatGPT's default model. The company says it improves answer quality, reduces hallucinations, and adds stronger personalization controls
featured_topics.why_it_matters: This is a direct platform-level model upgrade from OpenAI that can immediately shift user expectations, enterprise evaluation baselines, and competitive product roadmaps.
key_takeaways: OpenAI positioned GPT-5.5 Instant around smarter and more accurate responses, with explicit claims of reduced hallucinations versus the prior default ChatGPT experience.
The release includes both the product announcement and a GPT-5.5 Instant System Card, giving buyers and researchers an official safety and deployment artifact to review alongside the launch.
OpenAI said GPT-5.5 Instant improves personalization controls, a commercially important feature for retention and for tailoring assistant behavior across consumer and enterprise use cases.

evidence.title: GPT-5.5 Instant: smarter, clearer, and more personalized
GPT-5.5 Instant System Card

featured_topics.headline: Google Upgrades Gemini for Home to Gemini 3.1 for Multi-Step Smart-Home Commands
featured_topics.summary_short: Google updated Gemini for Home to Gemini 3.1 and expanded Google Home's ability to handle more complex, multi-step requests. Users can now combine multiple tasks into a single command
featured_topics.why_it_matters: Google is strengthening Gemini’s role as an ambient consumer assistant, a strategic battleground for device ecosystems, subscription upsell, and home automation lock-in.
key_takeaways: Google Home users can now issue more complex requests that combine multiple actions in a single command, moving the assistant closer to agent-like orchestration in the home.
The upgrade to Gemini 3.1 is intended to improve how Google Home interprets and executes requests, addressing a core reliability issue in voice-driven smart-home control.
Coverage described the release as Google’s biggest smart-home update since its 2025 AI revamp, indicating this is a meaningful platform iteration rather than a minor feature patch.

evidence.title: Google Home’s Gemini AI can handle more complicated requests
Google Home gets upgraded Gemini voice assistant and new camera controls
